title: 10 Natural Herbal Teas for Nerve Pain and Stress
intro_desc: Herbal teas for nerve pain are natural remedies that help alleviate discomfort and inflammation associated with nerve pain, a condition characterized by chronic pain caused by damaged or compressed nerves. The best herbal teas for nerve pain include Valeriana officinalis, Passiflora incarnata, Avena sativa, Lavandula angustifolia, Ginkgo biloba, Zingiber officinale, Hypericum perforatum, Cannabis sativa, Melissa officinalis, and Camellia sinensis. If left untreated, nerve pain can significantly impact daily life, leading to sleep deprivation, emotional distress, and reduced productivity.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis tea, also known as valerian root tea, has been used for centuries to help with nerve pain. This is because it contains a chemical called isovaleric acid, which has a soothing effect on the nervous system. When we experience nerve pain, it's like our nerves are on fire. But valerian root tea can help calm them down. It works by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation in the body. This makes it easier for the body to heal and reduces pain. Some people also find that valerian root tea helps them sleep better, which is also important for healing and reducing pain. When we sleep, our body repairs itself, and this can help with nerve pain.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Valerenic acid is good for nerve pain because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the nerves.
Valeranone is good for nerve pain because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the nerves, thereby providing relief from nerve-related discomfort.

Valerenal is good for nerve pain because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce pain, swelling, and damage to the nerves, thereby providing relief from chronic nerve pain.

remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal teas for nerve pain
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Withania somnifera herbal tea, start by gathering 1-2 teaspoons of dried Withania somnifera roots or 3-4 teaspoons of fresh leaves. You can purchase these from a health store or grow your own Withania somnifera plant. Next, boil 1 cup of water in a pot and let it cool for a minute. Then, add the Withania somnifera roots or leaves to the water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. The longer it steeps, the stronger the tea will be. You can also add honey or sugar to taste, but make sure to use a small amount as Withania somnifera tea is naturally bitter. Once the tea has steeped, strain it into a cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth. Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help manage nerve pain, but remember to consult with a doctor before using it as a treatment, especially if you have any underlying health conditions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata tea, also known as passionflower tea, is a natural remedy used to treat nerve pain. The tea is made from the leaves and stems of the passionflower plant, which contains a chemical called flavonoids. These flavonoids work by reducing the activity of certain nerve cells, called neurotransmitters, that send pain signals to the brain. This helps to block the transmission of pain, providing relief from nerve pain. The tea also contains an amino acid called tryptophan, which helps to produce a chemical called serotonin in the brain. Serotonin is a natural painkiller that helps to reduce anxiety and stress, making it easier to manage nerve pain. Additionally, passionflower tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the body, further reducing nerve pain.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Flavonoids are good for nerve pain because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease pain in the nerves, promoting healing and relief from discomfort.
Quercetin is good for nerve pain because it has antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nerves, which can cause pain and discomfort.
Polyphenols are good for nerve pain because they have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce nerve damage and swelling, which in turn alleviates the pain.

Kaempferol is good for nerve pain because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and protect nerve cells from damage, thereby easing nerve pain and promoting overall nerve health.

remedies.image_alt: Passiflora incarnata herbal teas for nerve pain
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Withania somnifera herbal tea that helps with nerve pain, start by gathering a handful of Withania somnifera roots or dried leaves, also known as ashwagandha. Rinse the ashwagandha in a fine mesh strainer under cold running water to remove any dirt or impurities. Then, place the ashwagandha in a teapot or a heat-resistant cup and pour in boiling water, making sure the ashwagandha is completely covered. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, or until the water has cooled down to a comfortable temperature. Strain the tea into a cup using the fine mesh strainer, and discard the ashwagandha. You can add a sweetener like honey or sugar to taste, but it's also best to drink the tea without any additives to get the full benefits. Drink 1-2 cups of the ashwagandha tea per day to help alleviate nerve pain and promote relaxation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tea, also known as ginger tea, is good for nerve pain. This is because ginger contains compounds that help to block pain signals to the brain. The main compound, called gingerol, reduces inflammation and prevents nerve damage. When we have nerve pain, it's often because of inflammation or damage to the nerves. Ginger tea helps to calm down this inflammation and protect the nerves, which eases the pain. Ginger also contains a compound called shogaol, which is known to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties help to repair damaged nerves and promote healing. By drinking ginger tea, we can reduce nerve pain and discomfort, and feel more relaxed and calm.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerol is good for nerve pain because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the nerves, thereby alleviating pain and discomfort.
Shogaol is good for nerve pain because it blocks pain signals in the brain by interfering with the activity of an enzyme called cyclooxygenase, which helps to reduce inflammation and relieve pain.

Polyphenols are good for nerve pain because they have antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and protect nerve cells from damage, making them a potential natural remedy for managing chronic nerve pain.

remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal teas for nerve pain
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Withania somnifera herbal tea that helps with nerve pain, you will need dried Withania somnifera root or leaves, boiling water, and a tea infuser or a cup. Start by adding one teaspoon of dried Withania somnifera root or leaves to the tea infuser or directly to the cup. Pour boiling water over the Withania somnifera, making sure the water level is above the herbal ingredients. Let it steep for 5-10 minutes, allowing the active compounds to infuse into the water. Strain the tea into another cup if using loose leaves or remove the infuser, and discard the solids. Your Withania somnifera tea is now ready to drink. It is recommended to drink it 2-3 times a day for nerve pain relief, and you can add honey or lemon to taste. However, consult with a healthcare professional before using it, especially if you are pregnant, breastfeeding, or taking any medications.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa tea is a good choice for people with nerve pain. This is because it contains compounds that help to calm and soothe the nervous system. The active ingredients in Avena sativa, such as avenacosides and avenanthramides, have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and pain in the nerves. When consumed as a tea, these compounds can help to block the pain signals sent to the brain, reducing the sensation of pain. Avena sativa tea may also have a sedative effect, helping to promote relaxation and reduce stress, which are common causes of nerve pain. Additionally, it can help to improve sleep quality, which is essential for nerve health and repair. By reducing inflammation, blocking pain signals, promoting relaxation, and improving sleep, Avena sativa tea may provide relief from nerve pain and help to improve overall nerve function.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Avenanthramides are good for nerve pain because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and block the pain signals sent to the brain.

Ferulic acid is good for nerve pain because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the nerves by blocking the production of chemicals that cause inflammation and pain.
Saponins are good for nerve pain because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and block the pain signals sent to the brain, providing relief from nerve pain.
Fisetin is good for nerve pain because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the nerves, and also blocks the production of pain-causing chemicals in the body.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Withania somnifera herbal teas that help with nerve pain, start by gathering dried roots of the Ashwagandha plant. Begin by boiling one cup of water and then reduce the heat to a simmer. Next, add one teaspoon of the dried roots to the water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. The longer it steeps, the stronger the flavor will be. Strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup, pressing on the solids to get all the liquid out. Discard the solids and add honey or sugar if you prefer a sweeter taste. Let the tea cool down a bit before drinking, as it is best consumed when warm but not boiling. Withania somnifera herbal teas can help alleviate nerve pain by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cannabis sativa tea is made from the leaves and flowers of the cannabis plant. It contains a chemical called cannabidiol (CBD), which helps to reduce nerve pain. When we feel pain, our nerves send a signal to the brain, which can be like a loud alarm. CBD in cannabis sativa tea helps to turn down the volume on this alarm, so the pain is not as strong. It also helps to reduce inflammation, which is when the body's immune system sends extra help to the area where there is pain. This extra help can sometimes make the pain worse. By reducing inflammation, CBD in cannabis sativa tea can help to make the pain less. Some people find that cannabis sativa tea helps with nerve pain because of its anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Cbd is good for nerve pain because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and discomfort in the nerves, while also promoting relaxation and reducing stress to alleviate pain.

Thc is good for nerve pain because it helps to reduce inflammation, block pain signals, and promote relaxation in the nervous system, which can help to alleviate chronic nerve pain and improve overall well-being.
Cbn is good for nerve pain because it helps to reduce inflammation and promotes healing in the nervous system, which can help to block the pain signals to the brain.
Terpenes have been found to help reduce nerve pain by acting as a natural analgesic, blocking pain signals to the brain, and also by reducing inflammation that can cause nerve damage.

Cbg is good for nerve pain because it helps to reduce inflammation, block the production of pain-causing chemicals, and increase the levels of neurotransmitters that help to regulate pain, making it a potential natural remedy for nerve damage and chronic pain conditions.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Withania somnifera herbal tea, start by boiling 1 cup of water in a kettle or pot. While the water is heating up, take 1 teaspoon of dried Withania somnifera root powder or 2-3 grams of fresh root and add it to a tea infuser or a small muslin cloth. Once the water has reached a boil, carefully pour it over the Withania somnifera root in the infuser or cloth. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, which will help the roots release their medicinal properties into the water. After the steeping time has passed, carefully lift out the infuser or cloth and discard the solids. Strain the tea into a cup if any sediment remains. You can now drink the tea hot or let it cool down to enjoy it at room temperature. This herbal tea is believed to help alleviate nerve pain and promote relaxation, and it's recommended to consume 1-2 cups a day for optimal benefits.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum tea, also known as St. John's Wort, has been used for centuries to help with nerve pain. The active ingredients in this tea, such as hyperforin and hypericin, have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and ease pain in the nerves. This can be especially helpful for people who experience numbness, tingling, or burning sensations in their hands and feet. The antioxidants in St. John's Wort tea can also protect nerve cells from damage and promote healing. Additionally, the tea may help regulate the body's response to pain, reducing the sensation of pain and discomfort. Some people find that drinking St. John's Wort tea regularly can also improve their mood and reduce stress, which can contribute to nerve pain.

Hyperforin is beneficial for nerve pain because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and inflammation in the nerves, thus providing relief from nerve damage and associated pain.

Hypericin is good for nerve pain because it has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce nerve inflammation and repair damaged nerve cells, which in turn relieves pain and promotes healing.

After the steeping time is up, strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any loose particles. You can also add a sweetener like honey or sugar to taste. Once the tea has cooled down, it's ready to drink. It's recommended to drink this tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate nerve pain and promote relaxation. Some people also choose to consume it before bedtime to promote better sleep. However, consult with a healthcare professional before using Withania somnifera tea for medicinal purposes, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or take medications.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tea is a popular choice for nerve pain relief because of its unique properties. It contains flavonoids and terpenoids, which are powerful antioxidants that help protect the nerves from damage. These antioxidants also improve blood flow to the nerves, allowing them to function properly. This increased blood flow can help reduce numbness, tingling, and burning sensations associated with nerve pain. Additionally, ginkgo biloba tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation around the nerves. This can lead to a decrease in pain and discomfort. Ginkgo biloba tea also has a neuroprotective effect, which helps to preserve the health of the nerve cells and maintain their function. As a result, ginkgo biloba tea may be beneficial for people suffering from nerve pain conditions such as sciatica, carpal tunnel syndrome, and neuropathy.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for nerve pain because it reduces inflammation and oxidative stress, which can cause nerve damage and pain, and also has antioxidant properties that help protect and heal nerves.

Isorhapontigenin is good for nerve pain because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce swelling and damage to nerve tissue, thereby providing relief from pain and discomfort.
Bilobalide is good for nerve pain because it has antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and protect nerve cells from damage, thereby providing relief from pain and discomfort.

It's recommended to consult a healthcare professional before using Withania somnifera herbal tea, especially if you're taking any medications or have a medical condition.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia tea, also known as English lavender tea, is a natural remedy for nerve pain. This tea is made from the flowers of the lavender plant, which contains compounds called terpenes and flavonoids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and calming properties that can help to ease nerve pain. When you drink Lavandula angustifolia tea, the terpenes and flavonoids are absorbed into your bloodstream and reach your nerves, where they can help to reduce inflammation and calm the pain. The calming properties of the tea can also help to relax your muscles and improve your sleep, which can further reduce nerve pain. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce swelling and pain in the nerves, which can be caused by conditions such as sciatica and fibromyalgia. By drinking Lavandula angustifolia tea, you can experience natural relief from nerve pain and improve your overall well-being.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Linalool has been found to be beneficial for nerve pain because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce swelling and irritation in the nerves, thereby providing relief from pain and discomfort.
Linalyl acetate is good for nerve pain because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the nerves, leading to a decrease in nerve pain and discomfort.

Rosmarinic acid is good for nerve pain because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the nerves, allowing for a decrease in discomfort and relief from nerve damage.
Lavandulol helps to reduce nerve pain by blocking the release of certain chemicals in the brain that send pain signals to the body, providing relief from discomfort.

If using fresh roots, chop them into small pieces to help release their active ingredients. Next, boil 1 cup of water in a teapot or kettle. Once the water is boiling, add the dried or chopped roots to the pot. If using dried roots, let them steep for 5-7 minutes. For fresh roots, steep them for 10-15 minutes. You can also add a pinch of honey to sweeten the tea if desired. After the steeping time is complete, strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth to remove the roots. Let the tea cool slightly before drinking. It is recommended to drink this tea 2-3 times a day for optimal benefits in managing nerve pain.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa tea, also known as turmeric tea, has been used for centuries to help people with nerve pain. The main ingredient, curcumin, is a powerful anti-inflammatory that can reduce swelling and pain in the nerves. When we have nerve pain, it's often because of inflammation in the nerves, so curcumin helps to calm it down. Curcumin also has antioxidant properties, which help protect the nerves from damage. This can prevent further nerve damage and help the nerves heal faster. Additionally, curcumin can help to reduce pain signals that the brain receives, making the pain feel less severe.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Curcumin is good for nerve pain because it reduces inflammation and blocks pain signals in the nervous system, which helps to calm and soothe nerve pain.
Demethoxycurcumin is good for nerve pain because it has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and irritation in the nerves, which helps to relieve pain and discomfort.
Tannins are good for nerve pain because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the nerves by blocking the release of certain chemicals that cause pain.

Begin by boiling water in the pot, then add one teaspoon of dried ashwagandha roots for every cup of water. remedies.remedy_desc: Withania somnifera tea, also known as ashwagandha tea, is often used to help with nerve pain. This is because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling in the nerves, which is often the cause of pain. The tea also has antioxidants that can fight off free radicals, which can damage nerve cells and cause pain. Additionally, ashwagandha tea can help to relax the muscles and reduce stress, which can also contribute to nerve pain. Some studies have shown that ashwagandha can help to increase the levels of certain neurotransmitters, such as serotonin and dopamine, which can help to reduce pain and improve mood. By reducing inflammation, fighting off free radicals, relaxing muscles, and improving neurotransmitter levels, ashwagandha tea may help to alleviate nerve pain.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Withania somnifera herbal tea, also known as Ashwagandha tea, you will need dried Ashwagandha roots or leaves. Start by boiling one cup of water in a kettle or pot. While the water is boiling, add one teaspoon of dried Ashwagandha roots or leaves to a tea infuser or a small muslin bag. Once the water has boiled, reduce the heat to a simmer and place the tea infuser or muslin bag into the water. Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the Ashwagandha to release its active compounds into the water. After the steeping time has passed, remove the tea infuser or muslin bag from the water and strain the tea into a cup. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but it's recommended to drink the tea as is to get the full benefits. Withania somnifera herbal tea has been traditionally used to help alleviate nerve pain and promote relaxation, making it a popular remedy for people suffering from anxiety, insomnia, and chronic pain.

supplementary_best: For nerve pain, a combination of herbs works better than one. Mixing Valeriana officinalis with Lavandula angustifolia creates a calming effect that soothes the nervous system. Valeriana officinalis contains a chemical called valerenic acid, which reduces anxiety and stress that can cause nerve pain. Lavandula angustifolia, or lavender, has a similar effect with its calming and sedative properties.  However, this combination alone may not provide sufficient pain relief. Adding Zingiber officinale, or ginger, to the mix can help reduce inflammation and ease pain in the nerves. Ginger contains compounds called gingerols and shogaols that have anti-inflammatory properties.  To further enhance the pain-relieving effects, Curcuma longa, or turmeric, can be added to the blend. Curcuma longa contains a compound called curcumin that has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.  This combination of Valeriana officinalis, Lavandula angustifolia, Zingiber officinale, and Curcuma longa can provide a well-rounded approach to treating nerve pain by reducing anxiety and stress, easing inflammation, and promoting overall health.
supplementary_avoid: If you have nerve pain, there are some herbal teas you should avoid. One of them is tea made from the root of the Angelica plant, also known as Angelica archangelica. This herb can stimulate the nervous system and make nerve pain worse.  Another herb to avoid is the one from the root of the Goldenseal plant, known as Hydrastis canadensis. Goldenseal contains a chemical called berberine, which can increase the risk of bleeding and interact with certain medications. This can be a problem if you have nerve pain and are taking medication to control it.  Tea made from the root of the Goldenseal plant can also make blood pressure drop too low. Low blood pressure can make nerve pain feel worse.  Tea made from the bark of the White Willow plant, known as Salix alba, should also be avoided. White Willow contains a chemical called salicylic acid, which is similar to aspirin. Taking too much salicylic acid can cause stomach problems, which can make nerve pain feel worse.  Tea made from the root of the Dandelion plant, known as Taraxacum officinale, can also be a problem. Dandelion root can stimulate the kidneys and cause the body to lose too much water. This can make dehydration worse, which can make nerve pain feel worse.  Tea made from the root of the Devil's Claw plant, known as Harpagophytum procumbens, should also be avoided. Devil's Claw can make the body react to stress in a way that makes nerve pain worse.  Tea made from the root of the Licorice plant, known as Glycyrrhiza glabra, can also be a problem. Licorice root can cause the body to retain too much water, which can make blood pressure go up too high. High blood pressure can make nerve pain feel worse.  Tea made from the root of the Black Cohosh plant, known as Actaea racemosa, should also be avoided. Black Cohosh can stimulate the nervous system and make nerve pain worse.  Tea made from the root of the Dong Quai plant, known as Angelica sinensis, can also be a problem. Dong Quai can cause the body to lose too much iron, which can make anemia worse. Anemia can make nerve pain feel worse.  Tea made from the root of the Sassafras plant, known as Sassafras albidum, should also be avoided. Sassafras root can stimulate the nervous system and make nerve pain worse.
supplementary_other_remedies: Capsaicin, a compound found in chili peppers, can help relieve nerve pain by blocking the production of a chemical called substance P. Substance P is responsible for sending pain signals to the brain. When capsaicin is applied to the skin, it activates the nerve fibers, causing them to release more substance P. However, over time, this leads to a decrease in the production of substance P, resulting in less pain transmission. Topical creams or ointments containing capsaicin can be applied directly to the affected area to help manage nerve pain.  Boron, a naturally occurring mineral, plays a crucial role in the body's production of neurotransmitters, such as serotonin and dopamine, which help regulate pain perception. Boron deficiency has been linked to an increased risk of developing conditions like fibromyalgia, which is characterized by widespread muscle and nerve pain. Supplementing with boron may help alleviate nerve pain by supporting the body's natural pain-regulating processes.  Gelatin, a protein found in animal bones and connective tissue, contains a compound called glycosaminoglycan, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce nerve pain. Glycosaminoglycan can inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals, thereby reducing inflammation and pain. Consuming gelatin-rich foods or supplements may provide relief from nerve pain by reducing inflammation and promoting healing.  Magnesium, an essential mineral, is involved in many biological processes, including muscle relaxation and nerve function. Magnesium deficiency has been linked to an increased risk of developing conditions like migraines and fibromyalgia, which are characterized by nerve pain. Supplementing with magnesium may help alleviate nerve pain by relaxing muscles, reducing inflammation, and promoting better nerve function.  Aromatherapy with essential oils like lavender and peppermint can also help relieve nerve pain. These oils have a calming effect on the body, reducing stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate nerve pain. When inhaled, the essential oils can bind to receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and pain relief.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with nerve pain often experience other ailments like anxiety, depression, and sleep disorders. This is because nerve pain can affect the brain's ability to regulate emotions and sleep. Medicinal herbs like passionflower and valerian root can help with anxiety and sleep disorders. Passionflower has a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and restlessness. Valerian root has a sedative effect, helping people fall asleep faster and sleep more soundly.  Some people with nerve pain may also experience digestive problems like irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). This is because nerve pain can affect the nerves in the digestive system, leading to symptoms like abdominal pain, bloating, and changes in bowel movements. Medicinal herbs like peppermint and ginger can help with digestive problems. Peppermint oil can relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing cramps and spasms. Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties, reducing inflammation and pain in the digestive tract.  Additionally, people with nerve pain may experience muscle spasms, numbness, and tingling sensations in their limbs. This is because nerve pain can affect the nerves that control muscle movement and sensation. Medicinal herbs like turmeric and cayenne pepper can help with muscle spasms and numbness. Turmeric contains a compound called curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. Cayenne pepper contains a compound called capsaicin, which can block pain signals to the brain, reducing numbness and tingling sensations.  Some people with nerve pain may also experience headaches and migraines. This is because nerve pain can affect the nerves that control blood vessels and pain perception in the head and neck. Medicinal herbs like feverfew and willow bark can help with headaches and migraines. Feverfew contains a compound called parthenolide, which can reduce inflammation and pain in the blood vessels. Willow bark contains a compound called salicin, which has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.  Lastly, people with nerve pain may experience fatigue and weakness. This is because nerve pain can affect the nerves that control energy and movement in the body. Medicinal herbs like ashwagandha and ginseng can help with fatigue and weakness. Ashwagandha contains a compound called withanolides, which can reduce stress and increase energy levels. Ginseng contains a compound called ginsenosides, which can improve energy production and reduce fatigue.
